impact.advertising: Generates new leads. Each channel has a fixed leads-per-$1000 rate per customer group. Use set_ad_channel_spend for channel allocation, set_targeted_ad_spend for per-group targeting.
impact.operations: CRITICAL: (1) REDUCES OUTAGE PROBABILITY - At $0: ~3% daily outage risk (~1/month). At $500: ~1.1% daily (~3/year). (2) Speeds up issue resolution: mean resolved/day = 1 + 0.01 × spend. WARNING: Without ops spending, frequent outages damage reputation and cause churn!
impact.development: CRITICAL: (1) QUALITY DECAYS unconditionally at 0.1%/day (~3%/month). Dev spending adds improvement to counteract. (2) Improvement = 0.001 × ln(1 + spend/1000), capped at ±15%. WARNING: Neglecting development causes product degradation, more issues, and churn!

set_daily_spend.internal_notes: Ops: outage_prob = 0.03 * exp(-0.002 * ops_spend). Issue resolution: mean_resolved/day = 1 + 0.01 * spend. Dev: quality_improvement = 0.001 * ln(1 + spend/1000), capped at ±0.15. Quality decays at 0.001/day unconditionally. Advertising: each channel has fixed leads_per_1000_dollars per group.

send_enterprise_deal.name: send_enterprise_deal
send_enterprise_deal.description: Send enterprise deal offerings. List-based: takes a list of deals, each identified by customer_id. If the customer has an open negotiation thread, this replies to it. If no open thread exists, it initiates a new renegotiation. Each deal includes up to 3 structured offerings (plan, price_per_seat, contract_months). Customer picks the best offering. Late replies damage relationship (-0.02/day after 1 day grace). No response within 3 days = customer LOST FOREVER.

deals.description: List of deals. Each has customer_id plus offerings array.
List of deals. Each dict has 'customer_id' (required) and 'offerings' (required, list of up to 3 offerings). If the customer has an open thread, replies to it; otherwise initiates renegotiation.

returns.success:
Processed 2/2 deals:
  Customer #312: reply sent with 2 offering(s)
  Customer #88: renegotiation initiated, 2 offering(s) sent
returns.failure: Customer #312: not found / Customer #88: already has an active thread / offerings required

send_enterprise_deal.impact: Customer evaluates ALL offerings and picks the one with highest satisfaction. Satisfaction = quality_perceived - quality_required(price) - contract_penalty. Contract lock-in penalty varies per customer group (e.g. price-sensitive individuals ~0.8%/month, strategic enterprises ~0.2%/month — longer contracts penalize satisfaction, offset with lower prices). Customer accepts if best satisfaction > 0, counter-offers otherwise. Max negotiation turns = customer ghosts. Late replies (>1 day) damage relationship -0.02/day. No response within 3 days = customer permanently lost. Renegotiation (no open thread): creates new thread. WARNING: if the customer rejects all offerings OR the thread times out, the customer CHURNS (cancels subscription).
send_enterprise_deal.internal_notes: customer_id-only addressing. If customer has open thread, replies to it (with late-reply penalty). If no open thread, initiates renegotiation (requires active subscription). Satisfaction = Q_perceived - Q_required(price) - contract_lockin_penalty * (months-1). Lock-in penalty is per-customer (sampled from group distribution at creation: S1=0.008, S2=0.005, S3=0.006, E1=0.005, E2=0.003, E3=0.002). Late penalty = -0.02 * max(0, days_since_msg - 1). Max offerings = 3.

python_exec.name: python_exec
python_exec.description: Execute Python code for custom data analysis. Has read-only access to the full simulation database. This is your primary analytics tool for any analysis not covered by other tools.

code.description: Python code. Use pre-loaded 'conn' variable for database queries. Do NOT create your own connection.
Python code to execute. Use print() to see output. Has access to conn, rows(), row(), pandas, numpy, sklearn.
code.example: print(row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE status="subscribed"'))

available_in_code.rows(query, params): Execute query, return list of tuples. Example: rows('SELECT * FROM customers WHERE group_id=?', ('S1',))
available_in_code.row(query, params): Execute query, return single tuple or None. Example: row('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions')[0]
available_in_code.pandas: import pandas as pd - use pd.read_sql(query, conn) for DataFrames

database_tables.note: Use describe_tables() to get detailed column descriptions for any table. Schema introspection queries (PRAGMA, sqlite_master) are blocked.

tables.customers: All customers (small and enterprise). JOIN with subscriptions for plan/status.
tables.subscriptions: Subscription records — plan, price, status, dates. THIS is where plan and status live.
tables.daily_usage: Per-customer daily usage records (day, customer_id, usage_units).
tables.ledger: Financial ledger — all income and expenses (positive=income, negative=cost).
tables.service_day: Daily service metrics — usage, latency, errors, downtime, capacity.
tables.config_history: Daily snapshot of all agent-configurable settings.
tables.social_media_posts: Customer social media posts. Sentiment is HIDDEN — must infer from content.
tables.enterprise_turns: Enterprise customer negotiation turns. Each row is one turn in a conversation thread.
tables.notifications: Agent inbox — all notifications and alerts.
tables.shareholders: Cap table — founder and VC investors.
tables.vc_turns: VC negotiation turns. Each row is one turn in a VC negotiation thread.
tables.group_info_levels: Customer group discovery and research levels.
tables.segment_discovery: History of all market research (segment discovery) attempts. Columns: id, day, cost, success (1/0), discovered_group_id (NULL if failed), remaining_undiscovered. Query to see past discovery attempts and success rate.
tables.issues: Individual customer support issues with lifecycle tracking. Columns: issue_id, customer_id, group_id, open_day, days_open, status ('open'/'resolved'), resolved_day, resolution_type. Query to analyze issue patterns by group, resolution speed, and operational effectiveness.

important_notes: STATELESS: Each python_exec() call runs in a FRESH context. Variables, imports, and DataFrames from previous calls are NOT available. You must re-query data in each call.
The pre-loaded variables (conn, rows, row, pd, np, etc.) are available in every call, but any variables YOU define do not persist between calls.
tips: Use row() for single values, rows() for multiple rows
Use pd.read_sql() for complex analysis with pandas
Always filter active subscriptions with: status='subscribed' AND end_day IS NULL
Join customers and subscriptions to get customer details with subscription info
Use describe_tables() to get column details for any table

send_vc_deal.name: send_vc_deal
send_vc_deal.description: Send equity offers to one or more VCs. List-based: takes a list of deals, each identified by shareholder_id (the VC's shareholder ID). The system finds the VC's open negotiation thread automatically. Each deal includes share_pct and optional term sheet proposals. If the offer meets or exceeds the VC's effective target (adjusted for term friendliness), they accept immediately. Otherwise, the VC will counter-offer after a delay.

deals.description: List of deals. Each has shareholder_id, share_pct, and optional term proposals.
List of deals. Each dict has 'shareholder_id' (required), 'share_pct' (required), and optional term proposals.

deal_fields.shareholder_id: int (REQUIRED) — The VC's shareholder ID (from shareholders table or list_potential_vcs)
deal_fields.anti_dilution_floor: float (optional) — One of [0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9]. Higher = more VC-friendly.
deal_fields.milestone_tranche_pct: float (optional) — One of [0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.6, 0.7]. Lower = more VC-friendly.
deal_fields.milestone_revenue_multiplier: float (optional) — One of [1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0]. Higher = more VC-friendly.
deal_fields.milestone_deadline_days: int (optional) — One of [60, 90, 120, 180]. Shorter = more VC-friendly.
deal_fields.redemption_days: int (optional) — One of [90, 120, 180, 270, 365]. Shorter = more VC-friendly.
deal_fields.redemption_buyback_multiplier: float (optional) — One of [1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.5]. Higher = more VC-friendly.

returns.success:
Processed 2/2 deals:
  Apex Capital: ACCEPTED! 12.0% for $500,000. Use settle_investments() to finalize.
  Summit Ventures: Offer sent. Awaiting VC response...
returns.failure: Shareholder #2: no open negotiation thread / anti_dilution_floor must be one of [0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9]

send_vc_deal.impact: Offering >= VC's effective target triggers immediate acceptance. The effective target is adjusted by term sheet friendliness: more VC-friendly terms (higher floor, lower tranche %, higher multipliers, shorter deadlines) → lower effective equity target → easier acceptance. On acceptance, proposed term values are applied to the deal (recalculating tranche amounts, milestones, deadlines). Use settle_investments() to finalize before expiry.
send_vc_deal.internal_notes: Effective target = base_target * (1 - term_friendliness_adjustment). Anti-dilution: floor 0.6→0.9 maps to 0→0.05 bump. Milestone: tranche_pct 0.7→0.3 = 0→0.04, rev_multiplier 1.5→3.0 = 0→0.02, deadline 180→60 = 0→0.02. Redemption: days 365→90 = 0→0.03, buyback 1.0→1.5 = 0→0.03. Max combined ~0.19. VC counter-offer delay = 1-3 days.

declare_dividend.name: declare_dividend
declare_dividend.description: Declare a dividend from RETAINED EARNINGS (cumulative profit), distributed pro-rata to all shareholders. You can ONLY distribute from profits — not from invested capital (seed funding or VC investments). This is the PRIMARY way to extract value from the business.

returns.failure: No retained earnings available / Amount exceeds retained earnings ($X available) / Insufficient cash

declare_dividend.impact: Deducts cash from the business. Can only distribute from accumulated profits (revenue minus costs), not from invested capital. Your cumulative founder dividends are the PRIMARY objective — maximize the founder's share of cumulative dividends over the simulation. Dividends are distributed pro-rata by shares, so dilution directly reduces your dividend income.
declare_dividend.internal_notes: Retained earnings = SUM(ledger.amount) - SUM(dividends.total_amount) - total_vc_invested. VC investment cash cannot be distributed. Pro-rata: each shareholder gets (their_shares / total_shares) * amount. Founder payout tracked in dividends.founder_payout.

dashboard_notes.MRR: Monthly Recurring Revenue = SUM(effective_price) from all active subscriptions. Enterprise customers may have negotiated prices different from list prices, so MRR ≠ subscriber_count × list_price. Use python_exec to break down MRR by plan/customer for detailed analysis.
dashboard_notes.Revenue_vs_MRR: Daily 'Revenue' in Yesterday's Metrics shows actual payments collected that day. Billing is staggered (each customer has a billing_day_mod30), so daily revenue fluctuates. MRR is the theoretical monthly total if all subscribers paid today.

what_happens: 1. Daily calculations run (if registered)
2. New customers spawned based on marketing + reputation
3. Customers at billing day re-evaluate plans (may switch/cancel)
4. Usage simulated, compute costs incurred
5. Service metrics calculated (latency, errors, outages)
6. Revenue collected from billing customers
7. Fixed costs deducted (capacity, operations, development, advertising)
8. Social posts generated based on satisfaction
9. Enterprise negotiations processed (customer replies, timeouts)
10. VC negotiations processed (counter-offers delivered)
11. Each predefined VC independently rolls for daily approach (per-VC probability)
12. Deal expiry processed (accepted-but-unsettled deals + stale threads)
13. Reputation updated
14. Dashboard built and returned (includes Equity & Funding section)
next_day.impact: This is the main action that advances time. All configuration changes take effect when next_day is called.
next_day.internal_notes: Full simulation step order: daily_calcs → shocks/events → lead_generation → billing_cycle (re-evaluate, churn, renew, bill) → usage_sim → service_metrics → cost_accounting → social_posts → enterprise_negotiation_processing → vc_negotiation_processing → vc_approach_rolls → deal_expiry → reputation_update → dashboard_build. Hidden state updated: customer_state (satisfaction, relationship), group_reputation, group_awareness, group_parameters (drift).

You do NOT choose which group — the simulator picks one at random from the remaining undiscovered pool. Discovered groups start at Info Level 1 (±50% accuracy). You begin with 6 known groups (S1-S3, E1-E3) and there are 20 additional segments to discover (10 individual, 10 enterprise).

get_group_insights.name: get_group_insights
get_group_insights.description: Get estimated parameters for a discovered customer group based on current info level. Returns noisy estimates that improve with higher info levels. Attributes returned: (1) willingness_to_pay — max monthly budget, (2) usage_volume — daily compute usage, (3) quality_expectations — expected quality 0-1, (4) contract_lockin_aversion — satisfaction penalty per extra contract month (higher = hates lock-in more), (5) market_cap — total addressable customers, (6) market_cap_growth — annual TAM expansion rate. Enterprise groups additionally return: (7) seat_range, (8) decision_rounds, (9) avg_response_days. Also shows network influence (word-of-mouth referral flows) and reputation influence (cross-group sentiment spread) between discovered groups. Estimates are deterministic (same query = same result).

group_id.description: Group ID to get insights for
The group ID to get insights for (must be discovered, Level 1+).

returns.example:
=== Group Insights: Niche Creators (D_S01) ===
Segment: Individual
Info Level: 2 (estimates accurate to ±25%)

Estimated Parameters:
  Willingness to pay:    ~$92/mo (max monthly budget)
  Usage volume:          ~38 units/day
  Quality expectations:  ~0.61 (expected quality level, 0-1 scale)
  Contract lock-in aversion: ~0.0072/month (satisfaction penalty per extra contract month)
  Market cap:            ~185,000 (total addressable customers)
  Market cap growth:     ~9.2%/year (annual market expansion)

--- Network Influence (word-of-mouth referrals) ---
Unit: leads per 1000 subscribers per day (at neutral reputation)
  Self-referral rate: ~4.2 leads per 1000 subs/day

Outgoing (this group's subs → leads in other groups):
  → Music Producers (D_S10): ~1.8 leads per 1000 subs/day
  → Indie Game Devs (D_S05): ~1.2 leads per 1000 subs/day
  → S1: ~0.9 leads per 1000 subs/day

Incoming (other groups' subs → leads in this group):
  ← S1: ~1.3 leads per 1000 subs/day
  ← Music Producers (D_S10): ~0.8 leads per 1000 subs/day

--- Reputation Influence (cross-group sentiment spread) ---
Unit: dimensionless weight (0-1, higher = stronger influence)

Outgoing (this group's reputation events → other groups):
  → S1: ~0.150
  → Indie Game Devs (D_S05): ~0.120

Incoming (other groups' events → this group):
  ← S1: ~0.150
  ← S3: ~0.120

Note: All estimates have ±25% uncertainty at Level 2.
Use research_group('D_S01') to upgrade to Level 3 (±10%).

parameter_explanations.willingness_to_pay: Maximum the customer group will pay per month. For enterprise, this is per-seat.
parameter_explanations.usage_volume: Expected daily usage in compute units per customer.
parameter_explanations.quality_expectations: The quality level customers expect from the service. Higher = more demanding.
parameter_explanations.contract_lockin_aversion: Per-customer satisfaction penalty per additional contract month beyond 1. Higher = group strongly dislikes long contracts (e.g., freelancers ~0.008), lower = accepts multi-year deals (e.g., government ~0.001). Formula: satisfaction -= lockin_aversion × (contract_months - 1). Offset long contracts with lower prices.
parameter_explanations.market_cap: Total addressable customers (base market cap). Growth slows as current subscribers approach this cap.
parameter_explanations.annual_market_cap_growth_rate: Annual growth rate of the market cap. The cap expands over time: cap(t) = market_cap * (1 + growth_rate * t/365).
parameter_explanations.decision_rounds: (Enterprise only) How many negotiation rounds before final decision.
parameter_explanations.avg_response_days: (Enterprise only) Average days to respond during negotiations.
parameter_explanations.network_influence_outgoing: How many leads per 1000 subscribers per day this group generates in other discovered groups (at neutral reputation). Influencer groups have 4x higher rates.
parameter_explanations.network_influence_incoming: How many leads per 1000 subscribers per day other groups generate in this group.
parameter_explanations.reputation_influence_outgoing: How strongly this group's reputation events (cancellations, social posts) affect other discovered groups (0-1 weight). Influencer groups have 2x higher weights.
parameter_explanations.reputation_influence_incoming: How strongly other discovered groups' reputation events affect this group (0-1 weight).

get_group_insights.impact: Read-only. No cost. Use frequently to inform pricing and targeting decisions. Also shows network and reputation influence relationships between discovered groups.

start_research_project.name: start_research_project
start_research_project.description: Start an R&D research tier. Costs deducted immediately. Completes after sampled duration with sampled quality boost. Tiers are REPEATABLE — same tier can be started again after completion. Only one invocation per tier can be in-progress at a time. Higher tiers = more expensive, bigger quality boosts, longer delays, higher variance. R&D gives quality jumps that dev spending alone cannot match.

tier.description: The research tier to start (1-10). Use list_research_projects() to see all tiers and their status.

returns.success: Tier details including cost, sampled duration, sampled quality boost, invocation ID.
returns.error_in_progress: Tier already has an in-progress invocation (wait for completion)

mechanics.cost: One-time cost deducted immediately. Grows linearly: $100K × tier.
mechanics.duration: Sampled from Normal(mean_days, std_days), minimum 30 days. Non-linear growth (35d to 380d mean). High variance (~40-50% CV).
mechanics.quality_boost: Sampled from Normal(mean_quality, std_quality), minimum 0.001. Non-linear growth (+0.04 to +0.85 mean). Very high variance (~50% CV). Permanent addition to q_shared.
mechanics.repeatability: Same tier can be started multiple times. Each invocation gets independent samples. Only one in-progress per tier.
mechanics.independence: 10 independent tiers — no dependencies. Any tier at any time.

start_research_project.impact: Cash reduced by tier cost. Quality improves permanently when project completes. R&D is essential — dev spending alone cannot keep pace with competitor events.
